pattern: Because you can only jump forward and the only thing that matters about a value is whether it is even or odd, keep a running best total for 'the path so far ended on an even value' and 'ended on an odd value' and update both as you scan left to right.
hints: You never need to remember which exact index you came from -- only whether the last value you collected was even or odd, since that is all that decides whether the next move costs you anything.
Track two running numbers as you scan the array left to right: the best total achievable so far ending on an even value, and the best ending on an odd value.

For each new value, it can either extend a path that already ended on the same parity for free, or extend the other parity's best path at a cost of x -- take the better of those two, add the current value, and use that to update the running best for this value's own parity.

edge_inputs.description: all even values, huge penalty never triggers
one odd outlier hidden among evens with a tiny penalty
parity alternates every step so every move risks the penalty
a large value of the opposite parity is not worth an enormous penalty
penalty is negligible so switching parity freely is always fine

stress.description: large random array with a random penalty, mixing parities
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    x = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    return [nums, x]
